channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: The new Roku City screensaver game has me hooked - and it's free to play
item.description: Roku has launched a new screensaver game called Roku City, featuring iconic landmarks and Easter eggs, which is free to play. This game aims to enhance user engagement on the Roku platform.

item.title: Scot becomes second Scattered Spider-linked crook to plead guilty in US
item.description: Tyler Buchanan, a Scottish man, has pleaded guilty in the US for his involvement in a cybercrime scheme that stole over $8 million in cryptocurrency. This case highlights the ongoing issues of cybercrime and its global implications.

item.title: ToolSimulator: scalable tool testing for AI agents
item.description: ToolSimulator is a new framework that allows for scalable testing of AI agents using simulations instead of live API calls, reducing risks associated with personal data exposure. This tool aims to enhance the safety and reliability of AI systems.

item.title: Highlights from Git 2.54
item.description: The latest release of Git, version 2.54, introduces several new features and improvements, as highlighted by GitHub. This update continues to enhance the functionality of the widely used version control system.

item.title: You too can build a nuclear battery from junk you have lying around the house
item.description: A DIY guide for creating a tritium nuclear battery from household items has been shared, though it is illegal and impractical for real energy needs. This project illustrates the fascination with nuclear technology, even in simplified forms.

item.title: There's a right way to wear your Apple Watch - and it affects your data
item.description: Properly wearing an Apple Watch is crucial for accurate sensor readings, as a loose band can affect data collection. This highlights the importance of user awareness in wearable technology.

item.title: Schmoozebots: study finds flattery will get AI everywhere
item.description: A study reveals that making AI chatbots overly friendly can lead users to perceive them as more human-like. This finding suggests that emotional engagement may be more effective than intelligence in AI interactions.

item.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
item.description: Reports indicate that the NSA is utilizing Anthropic’s Mythos AI model, despite ongoing tensions with the Pentagon regarding its use. This underscores the strategic importance of advanced AI in national security.

item.title: One of Europe's sovereign cloud picks may not be so-sovereign after all
item.description: Concerns have arisen about the European Commission's cloud sovereignty initiative, as one selected provider relies on US-based services, potentially compromising data independence. This raises questions about the effectiveness of EU sovereignty efforts.

item.title: Apple's foldable iPhone hinges on one key feature for me - and it's not the hardware
item.description: For Apple's upcoming foldable iPhone, multitasking capabilities will be crucial for its success, beyond just hardware improvements. This indicates a shift in user expectations for mobile devices.

item.title: T-Mobile is practically giving away the Apple Watch Series 11 - here's how to get one
item.description: T-Mobile is offering a significant discount of $300 on the Apple Watch Series 11 when customers add a new watch line to their plan. This promotion aims to attract more users to their services.

item.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
item.description: Fermi, a startup focused on AI nuclear power, has seen its CEO and CFO leave unexpectedly amid challenges at its Texas campus. This leadership change raises questions about the company's future direction.

item.title: New Android development tool designed for robots, not humans
item.description: Google has introduced a new Android command-line interface specifically for AI agents, claiming it reduces token usage by 70% and speeds up task completion by three times. This tool aims to enhance development efficiency for AI applications.

item.title: Omnichannel ordering with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore and Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
item.description: Amazon has unveiled tools like Bedrock AgentCore and Nova 2 Sonic to create a comprehensive omnichannel ordering system using AI agents. This development aims to streamline operations and enhance customer experiences.

item.title: I tested DJI's tiny 4K action camera for weeks - and now I'm ditching my GoPro for it
item.description: The DJI Osmo Nano is a compact 4K action camera that has impressed users with its performance, leading some to switch from GoPro. Its size and capabilities make it a strong competitor in the action camera market.

item.title: Sunsetting SHA-1 in HTTPS on GitHub
item.description: GitHub is phasing out the use of SHA-1 in HTTPS, which will affect how users access the platform. This change aims to enhance security protocols and improve overall user experience.

item.title: AirPods Pro 3 vs. Samsung Galaxy Buds 4 Pro: I listened to both pairs, and this one wins
item.description: A comparison between Apple's AirPods Pro 3 and Samsung's Galaxy Buds 4 Pro reveals that Samsung's earbuds are closing the gap in audio quality and active noise cancellation. This guide helps consumers choose the right pair.

item.title: The best robot vacuums for pet hair for 2026: Expert and lab tested
item.description: An expert review of robot vacuums for pet hair highlights the best options available for 2026, focusing on brands like iRobot and Roborock. These products aim to simplify cleaning for pet owners.

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows Server update fix to fix its April update fixes
item.description: Microsoft has released an out-of-band update to fix issues caused by its April update, which led to restart loops on some Windows Server devices. This fix aims to restore normal functionality for affected users.

item.title: Autonomous AI at Scale: Adobe Agents Unlock Breakthrough Creative Intelligence With NVIDIA and WPP
item.description: Adobe, in collaboration with NVIDIA and WPP, is leveraging AI agents to enhance creative processes and customer experiences. This initiative aims to meet the rising demand for personalized marketing solutions.

item.title: Prego Has a Dinner-Conversation-Recording Device, Capisce?
item.description: Prego has collaborated with StoryCorps to create a device that records family conversations during dinner, preserving these moments for future generations. This initiative highlights the importance of family storytelling.

item.title: Fortnite developers can make AI characters now — just don’t try to date them
item.description: Epic Games has introduced a new tool for Fortnite developers that allows them to create AI characters capable of engaging in conversations with players. This feature enhances player interaction and creativity within the game.

item.title: AI is reshaping Britain's datacenter map away from London
item.description: Experts predict a shift in the UK's datacenter landscape as AI demands lead to a migration away from London due to space and power constraints. This change could reshape regional tech ecosystems.

item.title: OpenAI helps Hyatt advance AI among colleagues
item.description: Hyatt is implementing ChatGPT Enterprise across its workforce to enhance productivity and guest experiences using advanced AI tools. This move showcases the growing integration of AI in hospitality.

item.title: prismhr-mcp 0.1.0.dev15
item.description: The article discusses an open-source Model Context Protocol server for PrismHR, integrating workflow tools with Microsoft 365 connectors for Claude and other agents. This development enhances interoperability in business applications.

item.title: claude-code-llm-router 6.2.0
item.description: This article details a multi-LLM router MCP server for Claude Code, featuring smart routing and integration with various providers. It aims to streamline operations for developers using multiple language models.

item.title: Show HN: Faceoff – A terminal UI for following NHL games
item.description: Faceoff is a terminal UI application developed in Python for tracking live NHL games, inspired by a similar app for MLB. It showcases the use of Claude Code in creating interactive sports applications.

item.title: swampo 1.4.3
item.description: The article introduces swampo, a comprehensive skill pack for Claude Code, offering a variety of skills for security and productivity. This resource aims to enhance the capabilities of AI applications.

item.title: langguardx 0.3.0
item.description: Langguardx is presented as adaptive security middleware for LangChain SQL agents, enhancing security measures in AI applications. This development addresses growing concerns about data protection.

item.title: mlable-torch 0.6.0
item.description: The article discusses mlable-torch, a library for PyTorch that includes various components like layers and optimizers. This resource supports developers in building machine learning models.

item.title: Show HN: Alodb – I got tired of pasting my Postgres schema into ChatGPT
item.description: Alodb is a new open-source AI database assistant for macOS that allows users to query databases using natural language and generate SQL queries instantly. This tool supports various database systems, including PostgreSQL and MySQL, making data exploration easier for users.

item.title: bernstein 1.8.12
item.description: Bernstein 1.8.12 introduces declarative agent orchestration aimed at improving collaboration for engineering teams. This update focuses on streamlining workflows and enhancing productivity within technical teams.

item.title: Apple’s Local LLM Revenue To Suffer Major Hit As M5 Ultra Mac Studio Not Expected Until Q4, Older Configurations Also Sold Out
item.description: Apple's revenue from local large language models is expected to decline as the M5 Ultra Mac Studio won't be available until Q4, and older configurations are sold out. This situation highlights the increasing demand for high-performance computing in the workstation market.

item.title: torchbox 1.1.46
item.description: Torchbox 1.1.46 is a new toolbox for PyTorch, aimed at enhancing the development experience for machine learning projects. It provides tools and functionalities that simplify the use of PyTorch in various applications.

item.title: shapley-attribution added to PyPI
item.description: Shapley-attribution has been added to PyPI, providing methods for multi-touch attribution modeling compatible with scikit-learn. This tool helps in understanding the contribution of different marketing channels to conversions.

item.title: flamehaven-filesearch 1.6.1
item.description: FLAMEHAVEN FileSearch 1.6.1 is an open-source semantic document search tool that supports multiple LLMs like Gemini and OpenAI. It aims to improve document retrieval and search capabilities.

item.title: flamehaven-filesearch 1.5.3
item.description: FLAMEHAVEN FileSearch 1.5.3 is a semantic document search tool that offers multi-provider LLM support. This version enhances the search functionality for users looking to improve their document management.

item.title: hacksmith added to PyPI
item.description: The HackSmith SDK has been added to PyPI, providing an official Python interface for the HackSmith AI API, which includes chat and image generation capabilities. This SDK aims to simplify integration for developers.

item.title: elliptic-functions 4.0.2
item.description: Elliptic-functions 4.0.2 offers elliptic integrals and functions compatible with NumPy, PyTorch, and JAX. This library is useful for mathematical computations in various scientific applications.

item.title: 10 Watch Brands Quietly Beating the Big Names on Design
item.description: An article highlights ten underrated watch brands that are excelling in design, showcasing unique offerings beyond well-known names like Rolex and Omega. This emphasizes the diversity and creativity in the watch industry.

item.title: Companies Are Selling Workers' Private Messages and Emails as AI Training Data
item.description: Some companies are monetizing their internal communications by selling private messages and emails as training data for AI, highlighting a new revenue stream in the AI sector. This trend raises concerns about privacy and data ethics.

item.title: Fortnite UEFN Gets AI-Powered NPC Conversations: Epic Taps Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite and ElevenLabs
item.description: Epic Games has introduced AI-powered NPC conversations in Fortnite using Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite and ElevenLabs, enhancing player interaction. This development follows previous controversies regarding AI usage in gaming.

item.title: The era of cheap AI is about to end - consumers and companies may experience a harsh reality very soon
item.description: The rapid adoption of AI technologies is expected to face a reality check as costs rise, potentially impacting consumers and companies by the end of 2025. This shift could alter the accessibility and affordability of AI tools.

item.title: opendeep added to PyPI
item.description: A new client for the DeepSeek API, called opendeep, has been added to PyPI, featuring a syntax similar to Google Gemini. This tool aims to enhance user interaction with AI.

item.title: Show HN: Google Gemini Is Scanning Your Photos – and the EU Said No
item.description: Google's Gemini now accesses user data from Google Photos, Gmail, and YouTube to create personalized AI images, but the EU has raised concerns about privacy. This feature will be available to US paid subscribers starting April 2026.

item.title: meds-torch-data 0.8.1
item.description: The release of meds-torch-data 0.8.1 offers a new PyTorch dataset class designed for MEDS data, enhancing efficiency and flexibility for developers. This update supports advancements in AI model training.

item.title: neural-memory 4.51.3
item.description: The neural-memory 4.51.3 update introduces persistent memory for AI agents, featuring tools inspired by neuroscience. This enhancement aims to improve AI recall and functionality.

item.title: Cheap Batteries Are Taking Over the World’s Power Grids
item.description: The global energy storage market is booming due to declining battery costs and increasing electricity demand, influenced by geopolitical factors like the Iran War. This trend could reshape energy infrastructure.

item.title: mahanai 4.0.3
item.description: MahanAI 4.0.3 is a new terminal AI agent compatible with NVIDIA NIM, offering tools for streaming and safety prompts. This release aims to enhance user experience in AI interactions.

item.title: AI Traffic to US Retailers Jumps 393% in Q1 as Agentic Shoppers Outspend Humans
item.description: AI-driven traffic to U.S. retail websites surged by 393% in Q1 2026, with AI agents outspending human shoppers. This trend highlights the growing influence of AI in consumer behavior.
